title: Aria and the Guessing Game
description: Help Aria discover how AI makes predictions by learning from examples.

steps.content: Aria loved guessing games. One sunny morning, she found three mystery boxes on her desk. One box had a picture of a sun, one had a rain cloud, and one had a snowflake.
Byte the little robot rolled in and beeped, “Can you teach me how to guess the weather?” Aria smiled. “I can teach you by showing examples!”

steps.question: If Aria shows Byte many sunny days with blue skies, what might Byte learn?
options: Blue skies often mean sunny weather
Rain always comes from blue skies

steps.content: Byte looked carefully at the examples. Blue sky, sunny day. Dark clouds, rainy day. White clouds and cold air, snowy day. Byte was not magic — Byte was learning from patterns.

steps.instruction: Which example would best help Byte guess that it might rain?
options: A bright sun with no clouds

steps.content: “Now I understand!” beeped Byte. “AI learns from examples and uses patterns to make guesses.” Aria nodded. “Exactly. But remember — guesses can be wrong, so wise people always check.”

steps.question: What is one smart thing to remember about AI predictions?
options: AI guesses from patterns and should be checked

steps.content: Aria gave Byte a golden star sticker. “Today you learned machine learning,” she said. “Tomorrow, we will learn how AI uses data!”
